flashcards.back: The value of the digit itself (0–9), independent of its position in the number.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between face value and place value?
flashcards.back: Face value is the digit itself; place value is the digit multiplied by its power of 10 (which depends on position).

flashcards.front: What is the face value of the digit 7 in 7,123?
flashcards.back: 7. Face value is always just the digit itself, regardless of position.

flashcards.front: In the number 333, do all three 3's have the same face value?
flashcards.back: Yes. All three 3's have a face value of 3, but different place values (300, 30, and 3).
